text:
DEPARTMENT OF PATHOLOGY AND LABORATORY MEDICINE
SURGICAL PATHOLOGY REPORT

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
PATIENT: Vasquez-Okonkwo, Renata
DOB: [on file]
SEX: Female
ORDERING PHYSICIAN: [Oncology/Surgery attending]
CLINICAL SERVICE: Surgical Oncology / Dermatologic Oncology
ACCESSION NUMBER: SP-XXXX-XXXXX
CASE TYPE: Surgical Pathology - Wide Local Excision and Core Needle Biopsy
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

CLINICAL HISTORY:
A 55-year-old female with a left posterior shoulder skin lesion, clinically suspicious for melanoma on dermoscopic evaluation, referred for wide local excision. Concurrent image-guided core needle biopsy of a hypermetabolic right axillary lymph node (identified on staging PET-CT) was also submitted. Patient has a history of significant cumulative sun exposure.

SPECIMEN(S) SUBMITTED:
  A. Left posterior shoulder -- wide local excision of skin lesion (WLE with planned 2 cm margin)
  B. Right axillary lymph node -- ultrasound-guided core needle biopsy (3 cores)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A (Left posterior shoulder -- WLE):
Received fresh, subsequently formalin-fixed, is an ellipse of skin and underlying subcutaneous soft tissue measuring 7.8 x 5.2 x 2.6 cm. The overlying epidermis bears a centrally located, irregularly pigmented macule-to-plaque lesion measuring 2.4 x 1.9 cm. The lesion is dark brown to black with focal areas of gray-white discoloration and an area of surface erosion/crusting measuring 0.5 x 0.3 cm centrally, consistent with ulceration. The surface of the lesion is flat to minimally elevated with irregular, asymmetric borders. Surrounding skin is unremarkable. The specimen is inked (peripheral margins blue, deep margin black) and serially sectioned perpendicular to the long axis at 3 mm intervals. On sectioning, the tumor invades deeply into the subcutaneous fat, with the leading edge at the deep resection margin measuring at least 4 mm clearance. The deepest extent of invasion corresponds grossly to the lower dermis/subcutaneous junction. No grossly apparent satellite nodules identified within the specimen. A total of 14 sections are submitted for histologic evaluation as follows:
  A1-A3: Full face sections through the geometric center of the lesion (perpendicularly sectioned)
  A4-A6: Sections through the thickest portion of the lesion with deep margin
  A7-A9: Lateral peripheral margins, left and right
  A10-A11: Superior and inferior peripheral margins
  A12: Additional sections of thickest area for immunohistochemistry
  A13-A14: Representative sections of uninvolved skin

Specimen B (Right axillary lymph node -- core needle biopsy):
Received in formalin are three tan-white core biopsy fragments, each measuring approximately 1.0 cm in length and 0.1-0.2 cm in diameter. Aggregate tissue: 3 cores. All tissue entirely submitted in one cassette.
  B1: All 3 cores entirely submitted

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:

Specimen A (Left posterior shoulder -- WLE):
Histologic sections reveal a malignant melanocytic neoplasm characterized by an asymmetric, poorly circumscribed proliferation of atypical melanocytes arranged in irregular nests and as individual cells extending along the dermal-epidermal junction (lentiginous and pagetoid patterns) as well as deeply invading the dermis and subcutaneous adipose tissue. The overlying epidermis demonstrates focal full-thickness replacement by atypical melanocytes. The intraepidermal component shows a superficial spreading growth pattern, with confluent and discontinuous nests of melanocytes at the dermal-epidermal junction, along with prominent single-cell pagetoid migration extending to the granular layer in multiple foci. In areas, the intraepidermal component extends beyond the dermal invasive component by several millimeters.

The dermal invasive component consists of sheets, fascicles, and irregular nests of epithelioid and focally spindled melanocytes with vesicular nuclei, prominent eosinophilic nucleoli, and moderate-to-abundant pale eosinophilic cytoplasm. Many cells contain fine to coarsely granular brown melanin pigment. Marked nuclear pleomorphism is present. Mitotic figures are readily identified, including atypical forms, with a count of 4 mitoses per mm2 (hot spot enumeration per CAP protocol). The tumor invades deeply through the reticular dermis and into the subcutaneous adipose tissue (Clark level V).

The Breslow thickness, measured from the top of the granular layer (or surface of ulceration) to the deepest invasive melanocyte, is 4.2 mm.

Ulceration: PRESENT. An area of surface ulceration measuring 0.5 mm in horizontal extent is identified, with overlying fibrinous exudate and neutrophilic infiltrate replacing the epidermis.

Tumor infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs): Brisk, nonbrisk pattern; a moderate band-like lymphocytic infiltrate is seen at the base of the tumor and focally infiltrating between tumor cells.

Regression: ABSENT. No areas of fibroplasia with melanophages replacing invasive tumor are identified.

Lymphovascular invasion: PRESENT. Multiple foci of neoplastic melanocytes are identified within vascular spaces in the reticular dermis, highlighted on D2-40 immunostaining (lymphovascular invasion confirmed; see ancillary studies).

Perineural invasion: ABSENT. No tumor cells identified in proximity to dermal nerves.

Satellite nodules: ABSENT.

Surgical margins:
  - Deep margin: Uninvolved. Closest deep margin: 4 mm (measured in A4).
  - Peripheral margins: All uninvolved. All peripheral margins are greater than 5 mm from the nearest tumor cell (closest peripheral margin: approximately 6 mm, lateral margin in A7).

Background skin: Scattered melanocytic nevi not identified in the adjacent sections. No evidence of regression in surrounding skin.

Specimen B (Right axillary lymph node -- core needle biopsy):
Sections of the three core fragments reveal fibrofatty and fibrous tissue with fragments of lymph node parenchyma. Within the sampled lymph node tissue, there is replacement of a substantial portion of the nodal architecture by sheets and cohesive nests of epithelioid tumor cells with identical morphology to the primary cutaneous melanoma (vesicular nuclei, prominent nucleoli, abundant pale cytoplasm, intracytoplasmic melanin pigment). The tumor deposit measures at least 18 mm on the largest cross-section within the biopsy material (note: core biopsy only, full nodal architecture not entirely assessable). Extranodal extension is present: tumor nests are seen extending through the fibrous nodal capsule and into the perinodal adipose tissue in multiple foci. No normal residual lymph node follicles are identified in the involved area.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:

Performed on both Specimen A (Section A12, representative invasive melanoma) and Specimen B (B1, lymph node biopsy):

  - S100 protein: POSITIVE (diffuse, strong, nuclear and cytoplasmic staining in both primary and metastatic deposits)
  - SOX10: POSITIVE (diffuse, strong, nuclear staining in both specimens)
  - HMB-45 (gp100): POSITIVE (cytoplasmic staining, stronger in superficial/junctional component; heterogeneous but present in invasive component and lymph node metastasis)
  - Melan-A (MART-1): POSITIVE (cytoplasmic staining, diffuse in primary and metastatic tumor)
  - Ki-67 (MIB-1): Approximately 35% nuclear positivity in the invasive dermal component and in the lymph node metastatic deposit (hot spot analysis)
  - D2-40 (podoplanin): Highlights lymphovascular invasion in dermal reticular component (Specimen A)
  - CD34: Highlights vascular channels; confirms intravascular tumor emboli in Specimen A
  - Pancytokeratin (AE1/AE3): NEGATIVE (excludes carcinoma)
  - p16 (CDKN2A): Loss of nuclear expression in the majority of invasive tumor cells (both Specimen A and B), consistent with CDKN2A inactivation

Note: BRAF V600E mutation-specific immunohistochemistry (VE1 clone) is POSITIVE in both specimens, demonstrating strong diffuse cytoplasmic reactivity, consistent with BRAF V600E mutation. Molecular confirmation by NGS pending (specimen submitted to molecular pathology).

PD-L1 IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY (22C3 pharmDx assay, Dako):
Tumor proportion score (TPS): 45%
Combined positive score (CPS): assessed; TPS is the clinically reported metric for melanoma.
Note: PD-L1 testing performed per institutional protocol for metastatic melanoma at time of initial diagnosis. Results are from Specimen A (primary melanoma, invasive component).

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES / MOLECULAR TESTING:

BRAF V600E IHC (VE1): Positive (see above)
NGS comprehensive solid tumor panel (523 genes): PENDING -- specimen from Specimen B (FFPE core biopsy) submitted to molecular pathology laboratory. Results to be reported under a separate accession.
Fluorescence in situ hybridization (FISH) for melanoma (RREB1/MYB/Cep6/CCND1 probe set): Not performed at this time; defer to molecular/NGS results.
Germline genetic counseling referral: Recommended given age and diagnosis; to be coordinated by clinical team.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSIS:

A. LEFT POSTERIOR SHOULDER, WIDE LOCAL EXCISION:
   INVASIVE MALIGNANT MELANOMA, SUPERFICIAL SPREADING TYPE
   - Breslow thickness: 4.2 mm
   - Clark level: V (invasion into subcutaneous fat)
   - Ulceration: PRESENT
   - Mitotic rate: 4 mitoses/mm2
   - Lymphovascular invasion: PRESENT
   - Perineural invasion: ABSENT
   - Microsatellitosis/satellite nodules: ABSENT
   - Tumor regression: ABSENT
   - Tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes: Brisk, nonbrisk (moderate)
   - Surgical margins: NEGATIVE (deep margin 4 mm; all peripheral margins >5 mm)
   - BRAF V600E IHC: POSITIVE (VE1)
   - PD-L1 TPS (22C3): 45%
   - pT4b (AJCC 8th edition: T4 = Breslow >4.0 mm; b = ulceration present)

B. RIGHT AXILLARY LYMPH NODE, CORE NEEDLE BIOPSY:
   METASTATIC MALIGNANT MELANOMA
   - Largest deposit: At least 18 mm (core biopsy; true nodal size not fully assessable)
   - Extranodal extension: PRESENT
   - Immunophenotype: Consistent with melanocytic origin (S100+, SOX10+, HMB-45+, Melan-A+)

OVERALL PATHOLOGIC STAGING (AJCC 8th Edition, pTNM -- based on available resected material; clinical N and M staging per radiology/multidisciplinary assessment):
   pT: pT4b
   pN: pN3b (clinically, given bilateral axillary and confirmed right axillary lymph node metastasis with extranodal extension)
   pM: pM1c (hepatic metastases confirmed on PET-CT; not pathologically sampled at this time)
   Overall stage: Stage IV (pT4b, cN3b, cM1c)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
COMMENT:
The morphologic and immunohistochemical findings are diagnostic of invasive malignant melanoma, superficial spreading type. The primary lesion demonstrates high-risk features including significant Breslow thickness (4.2 mm), Clark level V invasion, ulceration, elevated mitotic rate, and lymphovascular invasion. The right axillary lymph node core biopsy confirms nodal metastatic disease with extranodal extension. BRAF V600E immunohistochemistry is positive; definitive molecular confirmation by NGS is pending on a separate report. PD-L1 TPS of 45% by 22C3 assay is noted. Results have been communicated to the clinical team and discussed at multidisciplinary tumor board.

This case was reviewed and signed out by the attending pathologist.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Electronic signature: Attending Pathologist, MD
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine
Report finalized: [date to be added programmatically]
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------
